note: Result copy is clinical content (CLAUDE.md §9). Labels/tiers/support terms below are the ONLY approved strings (CLAUDE.md §2 rules 2–3). The disclaimer is verbatim from product plan §3.2 — do not paraphrase. The insufficient_evidence block (issue #22 minimum-evidence gate) is PLACEHOLDER COPY pending advisor sign-off — see docs/clinical-review/2026-07-02-minimum-evidence-gate.md. red_flag_confidence_note (issue #70) is likewise PLACEHOLDER COPY pending advisor sign-off — see docs/clinical-review/2026-07-09-recommendation-confidence.md. Worded as "area details" (not "area scores") since 1.3.1 so it also reads correctly when the flagged domain itself is gated to "Not enough information yet" rather than showing a scored confidence — see docs/clinical-review/2026-07-10-red-flag-confidence-note.md.
disclaimer: This is a screening tool, not a diagnosis. Only a qualified professional (paediatrician, psychologist, or developmental specialist) can diagnose autism.

insufficient_evidence.label: Not enough information yet
insufficient_evidence.explanation: This isn't a finding about your child — it simply means there aren't enough answers yet to share something reliable. We only reflect what your answers clearly show, and we never guess from too little.
insufficient_evidence.domain_detail: A few more answers about this area are needed before anything can be shared here.
insufficient_evidence.overall_detail: There aren't enough answers yet to estimate support needs or suggest a next step. Answer more whenever you're ready — everything you've shared is saved.

red_flag_confidence_note: This confidence comes from a clear, direct answer you gave — not from the area details above, which is why the two can look different on this screen.
